task_response.descriptor_evidence: contexts/01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 6 (main parts addressed but uneven, conclusion repetitive)

coherence.justification: The four-paragraph progression is clear, but cohesion is *mechanical* in the descriptor's exact sense: *"First and foremost / Secondly / In addition / However"* are stamped at sentence/paragraph fronts, and pronoun reference is *faulty* — *"they"* in *"Although they do not seem cost-effective"* and *"since they do have limitations"* lacks a clear singular antecedent (science). This matches `contexts/05-band-6-to-7/cohesion-upgrades.md` §2's "mechanical-linker trap" exactly.

lexical.justification: Topic-appropriate items present (*"sustainable energy system"*, *"reproducibility"*, *"scientific method"*) but precision and collocation slip: *"contribute to the issue"* reads as *worsen the problem*; *"natural energy"* should be *renewable energy*; *"reasonably high enough"* is tautological; *"use energy without concern"* is wrong register. Per `contexts/01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 7, "an awareness of style and collocation is evident" — that is what is missing here.

grammar.descriptor_evidence: contexts/01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 6 (complex structures attempted but error-prone; comma splice)
grammar.justification: You attempt a respectable variety — *Although*-clauses, relative clauses (*"which can lead people to use energy"*), participle hedges (*"given the fact that"*). However, the descriptor's gating signal — *"frequent error-free sentences"* — is not met: *"People did not fully understand when it comes to natural energy"* drops an object and mixes tense; *"nature is simply too vast to research, in other words, it is impossible to fully comprehend nature ever"* is a comma splice with adverb misplacement; *"every research"* misuses an uncountable. These are not communication-impeding errors, so it stays Band 6, not 5.

overall_rationale: All four criteria sit cleanly at Band 6 — the essay is relevant and organised, but development is uneven, examples are absent, cohesion is mechanical, and complex sentences contain noticeable errors. Per `contexts/01-band-descriptors/scoring-rules.md`, four 6s average to 6.0.
what_changed: The prior single-shot feedback was already on-target, so the bands stay at 6.0 across all four. Three substantive sharpenings: (i) I now flag the **uneven Body 1 vs Body 2 development** as the single biggest TR leak — Body 1 develops only one mechanism ("sustainable energy") while Body 2 develops two limitations, which is the opposite balance the prompt asks for; (ii) I add the **missing concrete example** problem (no Haber–Bosch, no falling solar-cost figure, no recycling case) as a separate fix from collocation work; and (iii) I add two grammar errors the prior missed: *"reasonably high enough"* (tautology) and the misplaced *"ever"* in *"comprehend nature ever"*. I also push two rewrites toward higher-leverage band-7 showcases (concessive `Adj+as+S+V`, complex noun phrase) drawn from `contexts/09-instructor-tips/07-band7-grammar-showcases.md`, which the prior did not exploit.

rewrites.original: Body 1 develops only "sustainable energy" with no example
rewrites.improved: Add a second offering with a concrete case: *"Beyond renewable generation, materials science is extending the life of finite resources: lithium-recycling pilots in the EU now recover over 90% of battery metals, easing pressure on virgin mining.
rewrites.reason: Fixes the structural imbalance that caps TR — the highest-leverage single fix.

rewrites.original: developing a sustainable energy system can contribute to the issue since it is possible to generate energy nearly infinitely
rewrites.improved: developing renewable-energy systems can directly mitigate resource depletion by **substituting** finite fossil fuels with effectively inexhaustible solar and wind generation
rewrites.reason: Fixes the *"contribute to the issue"* mis-collocation (sounds like worsening) and supplies a clear mechanism.

rewrites.original: In this essay, I will discuss what natural science can offer… and the limitations
rewrites.improved: While science can demonstrably extend the **effective stock** of natural resources through cleaner energy and material substitution, this essay argues that it cannot, on its own, resolve depletion, since the underlying drivers are economic and behavioural rather than purely technical.
rewrites.reason: Replaces an outline with a developed thesis — Band-7 TR requires a *clear and developed position*.

rewrites.original: However, it is naïve to believe that natural science could solve everything since they do have limitations.
rewrites.improved: Powerful as the scientific method is**, however, it has well-recognised limits that prevent it from resolving depletion in isolation.
rewrites.reason: Fixes pronoun mismatch and introduces one Band-7 *Adj+as+S+V* concessive inversion (one showcase per essay).

rewrites.original: human errors could happen in every research, and natural science is not an exception
rewrites.improved: human error can occur in **any research project** — for instance, the replication crisis in psychology and biomedicine has shown how easily even peer-reviewed findings fail to hold up
rewrites.reason: Fixes the uncountable *"every research"* and adds a concrete example, lifting both LR and TR.

rewrites.original: the chance of finding alternative ways of generating energy is reasonably high enough
rewrites.improved: the prospects for further breakthroughs in alternative generation remain genuinely promising
rewrites.reason: Removes the *"reasonably high enough"* tautology and replaces *"chance"* with the more precise *"prospects"*.

rewrites.original: People did not fully understand when it comes to natural energy, because of the complexity of how nature works.
rewrites.improved: Scientists still do not fully understand how to harness natural systems efficiently, **largely because** ecological and physical processes interact in ways that resist simple modelling.
rewrites.reason: Fixes the dropped object, tense, and *"natural energy"* miscollocation; adds a precise causal connector.

rewrites.original: nature is simply too vast to research, in other words, it is impossible to fully comprehend nature ever
rewrites.improved: nature is **too complex to be fully understood**; in other words, scientific knowledge of natural systems will always remain incomplete.
rewrites.reason: Fixes the comma splice and the misplaced *"ever"*.

rewrites.original: First and foremost… Secondly…" sequencers fronting both limitation points
rewrites.improved: One unavoidable limitation** is human error… **A second, more fundamental constraint** is the sheer complexity of natural systems…
rewrites.reason: Replaces mechanical sequencers with paragraph-internal lexical signposts — straight Band-7 cohesion.

rewrites.original: To sum up, although natural science has the potential to solve global challenges, it is not possible to solve every problem.
rewrites.improved: On balance, science is indispensable for **easing the supply side** of resource depletion through cleaner energy and material innovation, but the demand side — consumption patterns, regulation, and equitable distribution — lies beyond its reach. The most defensible policy mix therefore treats science as one lever among several.
rewrites.reason: Synthesising conclusion: re-states position in fresh wording and adds an implication, as required for Band-7 TR.

convergence_note: The prior chain has only one short feedback, so the convergence rules require `REFINING` regardless of confidence. Substantively, this iteration also adds three new diagnostic findings (paragraph-imbalance, *reasonably high enough* tautology, missing concrete examples as a separate fix) and a new band-7 showcase rewrite (concessive `Adj+as+S+V`) — meaningful enough that another pass could still tighten the rewrite ranking and citation precision.

# Iteration 08 — claude

_Generated 2026-05-10T10:23:40Z. Prior chain depth: up to 3. Manifest: 462 files._

---I have enough corpus context. Reading the essay critically against descriptors and producing iteration 2.

## Per-criterion scores
| Criterion | Band | Descriptor evidence (file path) |
|---|---|---|
| Task Response | 6.0 | `contexts/01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 6 (main parts addressed but uneven, conclusion repetitive) |
| Coherence and Cohesion | 6.0 | `contexts/01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 6 (mechanical sequencers, faulty reference) |
| Lexical Resource | 6.0 | `contexts/01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 6 (adequate but imprecise; collocation errors) |
| Grammatical Range & Accuracy | 6.0 | `contexts/01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 6 (complex structures attempted but error-prone; comma splice) |
| **Overall** | **6.0** | rounded per `contexts/01-band-descriptors/scoring-rules.md` (4×6 → 6.0) |

## What changed from prior feedback
The prior single-shot feedback was already on-target, so the bands stay at 6.0 across all four. Three substantive sharpenings: (i) I now flag the **uneven Body 1 vs Body 2 development** as the single biggest TR leak — Body 1 develops only one mechanism ("sustainable energy") while Body 2 develops two limitations, which is the opposite balance the prompt asks for; (ii) I add the **missing concrete example** problem (no Haber–Bosch, no falling solar-cost figure, no recycling case) as a separate fix from collocation work; and (iii) I add two grammar errors the prior missed: *"reasonably high enough"* (tautology) and the misplaced *"ever"* in *"comprehend nature ever"*. I also push two rewrites toward higher-leverage band-7 showcases (concessive `Adj+as+S+V`, complex noun phrase) drawn from `contexts/09-instructor-tips/07-band7-grammar-showcases.md`, which the prior did not exploit.

## Structural feedback
You address both halves of the prompt — what science offers, and its limits — so TR is not capped at 5. The headline structural problem is **paragraph imbalance**: Body 1 advances only the "sustainable energy" idea and then drifts into hedged speculation ("breakthroughs could happen at some point"), while Body 2 develops two distinct limitation points (human error, vastness of nature). For a discussion-style prompt asking equally about offerings and limits, the two bodies should be roughly symmetrical. **The single highest-impact fix is rewriting Body 1 to carry two developed offerings — e.g. (a) sustainable/renewable energy and (b) materials science / recycling and substitution for depleting metals — each with a concrete example.** Without that, TR cannot reach 7 because `contexts/01-band-descriptors/band-6-vs-7-comparison.md` requires *all main parts addressed with equivalent depth*. The conclusion is currently a one-sentence restatement of the intro; per `contexts/05-band-6-to-7/task-response-upgrades.md` §9, a band-7 conclusion synthesises (e.g. "science can extend supply but cannot remove demand pressures, so it must be paired with policy and behavioural change"). Paragraphing itself is logical and 4-paragraph shape is appropriate.

## Focus areas
- **Develop each body paragraph with a concrete, named example** — your essay has zero specific cases (no country, no technology, no figure). This is the most reliable Band-6 → Band-7 lever per `contexts/05-band-6-to-7/top-30-fixes-band-6-to-7.md` §10. Drill: `contexts/10-task2-topic-banks/topic-17-science-and-research.md` §"Sample fully-developed body paragraph #1", `contexts/05-band-6-to-7/task-response-upgrades.md` §6.
- **Pronoun reference (esp. "they" with science/research)** — *"natural science could solve everything since they do have limitations"* mismatches singular noun with plural pronoun; you do this 3+ times. Drill: `contexts/09-instructor-tips/10-common-korean-chinese-speaker-errors.md` §C3 distance subjects, `contexts/05-band-6-to-7/cohesion-upgrades.md` §3 Pillar 1.
- **Countable vs uncountable nouns** — *"every research"*, *"a result"* (where general "results" intended). Korean L1 transfer; flagged in `contexts/09-instructor-tips/10-common-korean-chinese-speaker-errors.md` §B2.
- **Comma splices in "in other words" reformulations** — *"too vast to research, in other words, it is impossible…"* needs semicolon or full stop. Drill: `contexts/08-vocabulary-grammar/05-spelling-and-mechanics.md`, `contexts/05-band-6-to-7/grammar-upgrades.md` §12.
- **Replace mechanical "First and foremost / Secondly / In addition"** with mid-sentence linkers and reference (`this limitation`, `such uncertainty`). Drill: `contexts/05-band-6-to-7/cohesion-upgrades.md` §4, `contexts/09-instructor-tips/08-cohesion-upgrades-instructor.md`.
- **One concession showcase per essay** — `Adj + as + S + V` (e.g. *"Powerful as scientific methods are, …"*) is a single high-leverage GRA upgrade per `contexts/09-instructor-tips/07-band7-grammar-showcases.md` §4.

## What's working
You hold a recognisable balanced stance — *"natural science can play a crucial role"* paired with *"it is naïve to believe that natural science could solve everything"* — and that even-handed framing is appropriate for the discussion prompt. Your topic vocabulary is genuinely science-aware: *"sustainable energy system"*, *"scientific breakthroughs"*, *"reproducibility"*, *"the scientific method"* are exactly the register `contexts/10-task2-topic-banks/topic-17-science-and-research.md` rewards. Keep the four-paragraph shape and the *"However, it is naïve to believe…"* pivot — that's a Band-7 move; don't lose it when you correct the surrounding errors.

## Per-criterion justification
**Task Response (6.0):** Both parts of the prompt are addressed and your position (science helps but is limited) is consistent. The Band-6 ceiling comes from three things: Body 1 develops only one mechanism, examples are absent throughout (only generalities like *"scientific breakthroughs could happen at some point"*), and the conclusion *"it is not possible to solve every problem"* simply paraphrases the introduction, which the descriptor explicitly flags as *"conclusions… repetitive"* (§Band 6).

**Coherence and Cohesion (6.0):** The four-paragraph progression is clear, but cohesion is *mechanical* in the descriptor's exact sense: *"First and foremost / Secondly / In addition / However"* are stamped at sentence/paragraph fronts, and pronoun reference is *faulty* — *"they"* in *"Although they do not seem cost-effective"* and *"since they do have limitations"* lacks a clear singular antecedent (science). This matches `contexts/05-band-6-to-7/cohesion-upgrades.md` §2's "mechanical-linker trap" exactly.

**Lexical Resource (6.0):** Topic-appropriate items present (*"sustainable energy system"*, *"reproducibility"*, *"scientific method"*) but precision and collocation slip: *"contribute to the issue"* reads as *worsen the problem*; *"natural energy"* should be *renewable energy*; *"reasonably high enough"* is tautological; *"use energy without concern"* is wrong register. Per `contexts/01-band-descriptors/task2-band-descriptors.md` §Band 7, "an awareness of style and collocation is evident" — that is what is missing here.

**Grammatical Range & Accuracy (6.0):** You attempt a respectable variety — *Although*-clauses, relative clauses (*"which can lead people to use energy"*), participle hedges (*"given the fact that"*). However, the descriptor's gating signal — *"frequent error-free sentences"* — is not met: *"People did not fully understand when it comes to natural energy"* drops an object and mixes tense; *"nature is simply too vast to research, in other words, it is impossible to fully comprehend nature ever"* is a comma splice with adverb misplacement; *"every research"* misuses an uncountable. These are not communication-impeding errors, so it stays Band 6, not 5.

## Top concrete rewrites (5–10, ranked by band uplift)
| # | Original phrase from essay | Improved (band-7 target) | Reason | Corpus citation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Body 1 develops only "sustainable energy" with no example | Add a second offering with a concrete case: *"Beyond renewable generation, materials science is extending the life of finite resources: lithium-recycling pilots in the EU now recover over 90% of battery metals, easing pressure on virgin mining."* | Fixes the structural imbalance that caps TR — the highest-leverage single fix. | `contexts/10-task2-topic-banks/topic-17-science-and-research.md` §Sample body para; `contexts/05-band-6-to-7/task-response-upgrades.md` §6 |
| 2 | "developing a sustainable energy system can contribute to the issue since it is possible to generate energy nearly infinitely" | "developing renewable-energy systems can directly mitigate resource depletion by **substituting** finite fossil fuels with effectively inexhaustible solar and wind generation" | Fixes the *"contribute to the issue"* mis-collocation (sounds like worsening) and supplies a clear mechanism. | `contexts/10-task2-topic-banks/topic-02-environment.md` collocations; `contexts/05-band-6-to-7/vocabulary-upgrades.md` §1 |
| 3 | "In this essay, I will discuss what natural science can offer… and the limitations" | "While science can demonstrably extend the **effective stock** of natural resources through cleaner energy and material substitution, this essay argues that it cannot, on its own, resolve depletion, since the underlying drivers are economic and behavioural rather than purely technical." | Replaces an outline with a developed thesis — Band-7 TR requires a *clear and developed position*. | `contexts/09-instructor-tips/06-task2-sentence-frames.md` §2 thesis frames |
| 4 | "However, it is naïve to believe that natural science could solve everything since they do have limitations." | "**Powerful as the scientific method is**, however, it has well-recognised limits that prevent it from resolving depletion in isolation." | Fixes pronoun mismatch and introduces one Band-7 *Adj+as+S+V* concessive inversion (one showcase per essay). | `contexts/09-instructor-tips/07-band7-grammar-showcases.md` §4 |
| 5 | "human errors could happen in every research, and natural science is not an exception" | "human error can occur in **any research project** — for instance, the replication crisis in psychology and biomedicine has shown how easily even peer-reviewed findings fail to hold up" | Fixes the uncountable *"every research"* and adds a concrete example, lifting both LR and TR. | `contexts/09-instructor-tips/10-common-korean-chinese-speaker-errors.md` §B2; `contexts/10-task2-topic-banks/topic-17-science-and-research.md` |
| 6 | "the chance of finding alternative ways of generating energy is reasonably high enough" | "the prospects for further breakthroughs in alternative generation remain genuinely promising" | Removes the *"reasonably high enough"* tautology and replaces *"chance"* with the more precise *"prospects"*. | `contexts/05-band-6-to-7/vocabulary-upgrades.md` §3 hedging toolkit |
| 7 | "People did not fully understand when it comes to natural energy, because of the complexity of how nature works." | "Scientists still do not fully understand how to harness natural systems efficiently, **largely because** ecological and physical processes interact in ways that resist simple modelling." | Fixes the dropped object, tense, and *"natural energy"* miscollocation; adds a precise causal connector. | `contexts/05-band-6-to-7/grammar-upgrades.md` §11; `contexts/08-vocabulary-grammar/01-grammar-error-correction-bank.md` |
| 8 | "nature is simply too vast to research, in other words, it is impossible to fully comprehend nature ever" | "nature is **too complex to be fully understood**; in other words, scientific knowledge of natural systems will always remain incomplete." | Fixes the comma splice and the misplaced *"ever"*. | `contexts/05-band-6-to-7/grammar-upgrades.md` §12; `contexts/08-vocabulary-grammar/05-spelling-and-mechanics.md` |
| 9 | "First and foremost… Secondly…" sequencers fronting both limitation points | "**One unavoidable limitation** is human error… **A second, more fundamental constraint** is the sheer complexity of natural systems…" | Replaces mechanical sequencers with paragraph-internal lexical signposts — straight Band-7 cohesion. | `contexts/05-band-6-to-7/cohesion-upgrades.md` §8; `contexts/09-instructor-tips/08-cohesion-upgrades-instructor.md` |
| 10 | "To sum up, although natural science has the potential to solve global challenges, it is not possible to solve every problem." | "On balance, science is indispensable for **easing the supply side** of resource depletion through cleaner energy and material innovation, but the demand side — consumption patterns, regulation, and equitable distribution — lies beyond its reach. The most defensible policy mix therefore treats science as one lever among several." | Synthesising conclusion: re-states position in fresh wording and adds an implication, as required for Band-7 TR. | `contexts/05-band-6-to-7/task-response-upgrades.md` §9; `contexts/05-band-6-to-7/paragraph-structure-band7.md` §5 |

## Overall band rationale
All four criteria sit cleanly at Band 6 — the essay is relevant and organised, but development is uneven, examples are absent, cohesion is mechanical, and complex sentences contain noticeable errors. Per `contexts/01-band-descriptors/scoring-rules.md`, four 6s average to 6.0.

## CONVERGENCE: REFINING
The prior chain has only one short feedback, so the convergence rules require `REFINING` regardless of confidence. Substantively, this iteration also adds three new diagnostic findings (paragraph-imbalance, *reasonably high enough* tautology, missing concrete examples as a separate fix) and a new band-7 showcase rewrite (concessive `Adj+as+S+V`) — meaningful enough that another pass could still tighten the rewrite ranking and citation precision.
